steps.title: Identify the interrogative structure
steps.content: The blank must complete a sentence beginning with How that asks a direct question. The following sentence, “A layer of microbubbles on their plumage reduces friction as the penguins speed to the surface,” is an independent clause providing the explanation.

steps.title: Match the interrogative form and punctuation
steps.content: Direct interrogative sentences require inversion of the auxiliary verb and subject: are they able to move not they are able to move, and they end with a question mark. D provides both—“How are they able to move so fast?”—making it the correct interrogative form that precedes and sets up the explanation in the next sentence. A and C fail the inversion requirement, while B has the inversion but uses a period instead of a question mark. That matches choice D — D is correct.
